item.title: Europe’s Diesel Shock Deepens as Supplies Head Elsewhere
item.description: Prices have now passed $200 a barrel, the highest since 2022.

item.title: Japan’s Dynamic Job Market Rebuts the Image of Demographic Decline
item.description: Japanese wage rises are unfolding amid an expansion of the labor force that rebuts the narrative of demographic decline.

item.title: Unilever’s $42 Billion Stock Wipeout Deepens on McCormick Deal
item.description: A transformative deal for Unilever Plc’s food division has upended the narrative for the shares, raising investor worries about a stock that has long been viewed as a defensive bellwether.

item.title: OpenClaw Unlocks China’s AI Token Export Business
item.description: Affordable Chinese AI services are surging in global usage because of OpenClaw

item.title: Kazakhstan’s Sovereign Wealth Fund Readies Debut Panda Bond Sale
item.description: Kazakhstan’s sovereign wealth fund is preparing a debut sale of panda bonds as soon as this month, according to people familiar with the plans.

item.title: How to Get a Tariff Refund
item.description: Thousands of businesses have a shot at getting some money back from the US government—but there will be a playbook.

item.title: Trump’s Tariffs Return to the Foe in Bid to Boost US Production
item.description: After his metals tariffs sparked a backlash, Trump aims to simplify the compliance process

item.title: US Fertilizer Traders Eye Windfall Abroad as War Upends Markets
item.description: American fertilizer traders have seized on a lucrative opportunity overseas because of the war in Iran, showing how US farmers have been caught again on the wrong side of moves in global markets.

item.title: Blackstone’s AirTrunk Is Said to Pick Banks for Singapore REIT IPO
item.description: AirTrunk, the data center operator Blackstone Inc. bought in 2024, has picked banks for a real estate investment trust listing in Singapore that may raise about $1.5 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Singapore to Boost Support as War Disrupts Energy Supplies
item.description: Singapore will enhance support measures announced in the budget and bring some of them forward to provide earlier relief as the Middle East conflict strains energy supplies.

item.title: Blackstone’s AGS Health Said to File for $500 Million India IPO
item.description: Blackstone Inc.-owned AGS Health Pvt. has filed draft paperwork for an initial public offering in Mumbai through the confidential route, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Gilts Drop With Global Debt Despite Bailey’s Caution on Hikes
item.description: Traders added to wagers that UK interest rates will rise this year, sending gilts lower, just a day after Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey said markets were getting ahead of themselves in pricing tighter monetary policy.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo Takes Stake in Beckham’s Health Firm IM8
item.description: Milwaukee Bucks star Giannis Antetokounmpo is taking an equity stake in IM8, co-founded by health-sciences firm Prenetics and former footballer David Beckham.

item.title: KKR Secures $23 Billion for Americas PE in Its Largest-Ever Haul
item.description: KKR & Co. raised approximately $23 billion for its latest Americas buyout fund, defying an industry slump as private equity firms struggle to sell assets and return capital to investors.

item.title: Clock Ticks for a Dozen Indian IPOs as Approvals Near Expiry
item.description: A wave of initial public offerings in India is at risk, with their approvals nearing expiry as a downturn of equities is making companies more cautions about coming to the market.

item.title: War Angst Is Causing Stock Selloffs Heading Into Weekends
item.description: Investors are curbing risk on Thursdays and Fridays.

item.title: BlackRock Bets German Bonds Will Be Hammered by Inflation Surge
item.description: BlackRock Inc. has added to bearish positions on German bonds, betting that a “pretty large inflation uptick” across Europe will push borrowing costs back above the 15-year highs reached last week.

item.title: How Swap Markets Are Struggling to Gauge Rates as Iran War Fuels Volatility
item.description: The Iran war has clouded the outlook for inflation and economic growth across the globe and, in turn, where interest rates might be heading.

item.title: UK Firms Lift Price Expectations on Energy Shock, BOE Says
item.description: UK businesses plan to push through bigger price increases in response to the surge in energy costs triggered by the Middle East conflict, according to a Bank of England survey.

item.title: Sudan Gold Exports to UAE Slump After Diplomatic Ties Severed
item.description: Sudanese gold exports to the United Arab Emirates plunged last year, after the military-backed government ended relations with the Gulf state.

item.title: Goldman Paris Is Warned of Iran Group Bomb Threat: Parisien
item.description: The headquarters of Goldman Sachs Group Inc. in Paris were placed under police surveillance Wednesday night following bomb threats believed to be from an Iranian group, Le Parisien newspaper reported.

item.title: Onetime Elon Musk Whisperer Shares the ‘Algorithm’
item.description: When Jon McNeill left Tesla in 2018, he took with him a reputation as a growth driver and fix-it man. If you can manage Musk’s special brand of chaos, the thinking goes, you can run anything.

item.title: ECB Encourages Banks to Test Lending Offers as Reserves Shrink
item.description: Banks should prepare to tap regular loan offers by the European Central Bank as quantitative tightening proceeds and liquidity sloshing around the financial system shrinks, according to a blog post published Thursday.

item.title: Spring Break Bills Skyrocket for Wealthy Americans Vacationing at the Beach
item.description: The Naples Beach Club, a Four Seasons resort on the Gulf Coast of Florida, opened in November as a lodging industry rarity: a new luxury hotel built directly on a US beach.

item.title: Neoen to Build France’s Largest Battery Amid Strained Power Grid
item.description: Renewable energy developer Neoen will build France’s largest battery, helping to store rising solar and wind generation and ease strain on the country’s grid.

item.title: ECB’s Panetta Says Even If Iran War Ends Damage Has Been Done
item.description: Damage caused by the US’s war against Iran will continue to have a negative impact on the global economy even if hostilities end soon, according to European Central Bank Governing Council member Fabio Panetta.

item.title: Swiss Franc Set to Reclaim Haven Role, Options Trading Suggests
item.description: The Swiss franc hasn’t been playing its traditional haven role of late, even as the war in Iran keeps geopolitics fraught. Options trading suggests the anomaly won’t last.

item.title: Hedge Funds Walloped by a Month of Turmoil Sparked by Iran War
item.description: Some of the world’s biggest hedge funds known for delivering steady returns lost money in March as the war in the Middle East roiled markets across energy, bonds and equities and forced traders to unwind crowded positions.

item.title: Volvo Says Iran War Hit US Demand, Signaling Wider Auto Pain
item.description: Volvo Car AB said the war in Iran hurt US demand in the first quarter, offering one of the clearest signs yet that the conflict is starting to dent sales as higher fuel prices curb spending.

item.title: ECB’s Cipollone Sees ‘Good Momentum’ for Digital Euro Project
item.description: European Central Bank Executive Board member Piero Cipollone sees “good momentum” for a digital euro, with a possible launch in July 2029.
